role_title: Senior Product Manager II, Personalisation & Data

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in product management
Role title: Senior Product Manager II
Operates with high autonomy on 15-month technical roadmaps

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in product management with a specific focus on data products, platforms, or ML-driven environments.
Demonstrated ability to perform independent data analysis using SQL/Python and define robust input/output KPIs.
Strong critical thinking skills used to identify high-impact model or data adjustments and manage stakeholder expectations.
Proficiency in translating complex concepts (e.g., data lineage, model cold-starts) into clear narratives for non-technical stakeholders.
Proven experience incorporating AI-assisted workflows into daily professional practice to enhance output quality and discovery speed.
Lead the iteration cycle for model testing, designing and executing complex A/B testing roadmaps to improve core metrics and reduce lead time to insights.
Translate high-ambiguity business requirements into long-term (15-month) technical roadmaps, operating with high autonomy to align data infrastructure with global commercial objectives.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Integrate GenAI tools into the product discovery lifecycle to accelerate the synthesis of customer signals and the delivery of decision-ready technical specifications.

scope.surface: Personalized Chef initiative — data products powering personalization, marketing, and operational excellence across 18+ business units
primary_metrics_inferred: Data asset quality and consumption readiness
A/B test conversion and improvement rates
core_responsibilities: Architect and deliver a strategy for Core Data Products, ensuring high-quality, connected data assets (customer profiles, subscription plans, order history) are ready for consumption by global stakeholders.
Lead the iteration cycle for model testing, designing and executing complex A/B testing roadmaps to improve core metrics and reduce lead time to insights.
Integrate GenAI tools into the product discovery lifecycle to accelerate the synthesis of customer signals and the delivery of decision-ready technical specifications.
Translate high-ambiguity business requirements into long-term (15-month) technical roadmaps, operating with high autonomy to align data infrastructure with global commercial objectives.
Act as the primary point of contact between technical engineering squads and commercial business units to ensure data ecosystem connectivity.

green_flags: Clear product surface and mission-critical domain (Personalized Chef initiative) described in detail
Explicitly encourages diverse backgrounds — 'we don't tick all the boxes' language
Promises to respond within 5 business days
Real-world scale stated: 6+ million customers, 18+ business units
Modern tech stack in an international team environment
red_flags: Role is framed as a 'service provider' and 'consultant' rather than a full-time employee — contractual nature is ambiguous
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned
Onsite Warsaw requirement may limit applicant pool; no hybrid or remote option stated
GenAI integration listed as a responsibility but not clearly categorized as required vs. preferred

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior PM II' and 5+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD body description of subscription-based DTC model)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite based on Warsaw office mention; no hybrid/remote language present)
nice_to_haves: GenAI integration classified as soft because it appears as a responsibility description, not explicitly flagged as required under ingredients section
ambiguities: The JD repeatedly refers to the role as 'service provider' and 'consultant' which may indicate a contractor or consulting engagement rather than direct employment — the nature of the engagement is not explicitly clarified.

GenAI integration appears both in responsibilities ('What you'll do') and implicitly in requirements ('AI Integration') — it is listed as a must-have under AI Integration but the GenAI tooling specifics in responsibilities were also captured as a nice-to-have to reflect possible overlap.
No explicit hybrid or remote language found; treating as onsite based solely on office access description, but this is not confirmed.
